files.policy.txt: Refund policy: customers may request a refund within 30 days when the purchase was made directly and the account is in good standing.
files.account/limits.txt: Account limits: starter plans include 5 seats, 10 projects, and 100GB of monthly transfer.
files.release_notes.txt: Release notes: version 2.4 improves cache invalidation, adds audit logging, and fixes invoice export retries.

long_support.content: You are a careful support assistant. Preserve account facts and answer with practical next steps.
Thanks Riley, I will keep that in mind.
Understood. I will refer to your company as Northstar Analytics.
Got it, Apollo Ledger is the project name.
Earlier we discussed authentication errors after rotating service tokens in staging. The symptoms included intermittent 401s, delayed cache propagation, and an outdated deployment note.
I suggested checking token TTL, deployment order, and whether all workers received the new signing key.
Then we reviewed billing behavior. The account has annual invoicing, two pending seat changes, and a finance approval workflow that requires purchase order references.
I recommended grouping seat changes into the next renewal invoice and attaching the purchase order to the billing ticket.
remember the customer prefers direct answers with no marketing language
I will keep the tone direct and avoid marketing phrasing.
We also explored an export issue where CSV downloads were timing out for large date ranges. The workaround was to split exports by month until the background job limit was increased.
The safest workaround was monthly exports, then retrying the background job after the limit increase.
The latest message from the customer says they still see one failed export after reducing the date range and want to know what to try next.
Ask for the export job id, confirm the reduced date range, and check whether the failed job hit the old timeout limit.

long_document.content: You help analyze internal documents. Retain instructions, important facts, and the latest request.
I will remember your name is Jordan Lee.
remember that summaries should call out owners, dates, and risks
I will include owners, dates, and risks when summarizing.
The document draft describes a Q3 migration from a legacy queue to a managed event bus. It mentions retries, dead-letter queues, audit log retention, and a phased rollout by region.
The key operational topics are retries, dead-letter handling, audit retention, and regional rollout.
A later section says compliance review is owned by Priya, infrastructure review is owned by Marco, and customer communications are owned by Elena.
Owner mapping recorded: Priya for compliance, Marco for infrastructure, Elena for communications.
The risk register includes delayed vendor approval, insufficient replay testing, and unclear rollback criteria for the first production region.
The major risks are vendor approval delay, replay testing gaps, and rollback criteria ambiguity.
The final section says the decision meeting is scheduled for August 14 and the migration freeze begins September 3.
Important dates: decision meeting August 14, migration freeze September 3.

notes.invoice_policy: Invoice processing notes: enterprise customers require purchase order matching, tax-id validation, and approval from finance when annual contract value changes. Refunds are handled separately from credits. Credits can be applied only to open invoices, and failed payment retries run after 24 hours, 72 hours, and seven days.
notes.security_policy: Security review notes: high-risk changes require audit logging, owner approval, rollback instructions, and staged deployment evidence. Access-token changes must include rotation timing, impacted service list, and validation steps. Customer-facing notices must avoid implementation secrets.
